description_long: This is a large commercial passenger airplane with a streamlined fuselage painted primarily in white, featuring a red, blue, and gray tail fin with a stylized flag design. The aircraft has two large turbofan engines mounted under its low, swept-back wings, which are equipped with subtle upward-angled winglets. The nose is rounded and painted red at the tip, and the cockpit windows are clearly visible at the front. The landing gear is not visible, suggesting it is likely retractable and stowed. The livery includes the 'BRITISH AIRWAYS' branding along the forward fuselage, and the overall design is consistent with modern wide-body airliners used for long-haul flights.
description: A modern, white commercial airliner with red, blue, and gray tail markings, twin underwing turbofan engines, and 'BRITISH AIRWAYS' branding. The airplane features swept-back low wings and a streamlined fuselage.
description_view_0: Rear view showing the red tail fin, horizontal stabilizers, and the full wingspan with visible engine nacelles under the wings.
description_view_1: Left side profile displaying the 'BRITISH AIRWAYS' branding, cockpit windows, and the detailed tail fin design.
description_view_2: Front view highlighting the rounded red nose, cockpit windows, and the two large underwing engines.
description_view_3: Right side profile similar to the left, showing the tail fin, engines, and fuselage details.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing, swept-back wings with subtle upward-angled winglets and moderate dihedral angle.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Retractable tricycle landing gear (not visible, inferred from typical configuration and absence in images).
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed multi-seat cockpit with multiple windows at the front of the fuselage.
